candidates.title: Mockup images for website
candidates.brief: Premium App & Product Mockup Imagery — Australian Preventative Health Platform About Vively Vively is a diagnostics-led preventative health platform combining at-home blood testing, continuous glucose monitoring, wearable integration, and AI-guided coaching with practitioner support. We sit in the same category-defining space as Function Health (US), Levels, Oura, and Whoop. Our customers are health-conscious Australians paying a premium for a unified, science-backed health system. We're upgrading our website imagery and need a designer who can produce world-class mockup visuals that match the quality bar set by the brands above. What We Need A set of high-end mockup compositions to use across our marketing website (hero sections, feature blocks, pricing pages, social ads). Imagery must feel premium, modern, scientific, and warm — not stocky, not generic SaaS, not clinical-cold. Delivera

candidates.title: Graphic designer needed: Uber Eats banner for a NY style sandwich brand
candidates.brief: CRUMB is a NY-style sandwich shop based in Leppington, Western Sydney. We're gritty, bold, and a little chaotic — think New York deli meets Nonna's kitchen meets street culture. Not polished or corporate. We need a banner for our Uber Eats page. We have the assets, they just need to be composited together so they look like they were shot in the same image. The result should feel like one cohesive, intentional photo, not a collage. Look us up before you apply. We have a strong visual identity and we need someone who gets it - @crumbsydney on IG Who we're looking for Strong photo compositing and retouching skills Experience with lifestyle and food brands An eye for gritty, character-led aesthetics (not stock photo) Fast turnaround - we're aiming to have this live within the week The deliverable 1 x Uber Eats banner Margins must fill the expected dimension (2880 pixel width and 2304 pixel h

candidates.title: Luxury Travel Itinerary Design
candidates.tools: Photoshop · Illustrator · InDesign — for print-ready CMYK output and layered/retouched assets
candidates.brief: We’re refreshing the way we present our bespoke itineraries to guests— from the downloadable PDF they receive before departure, to the teaser images we share on social channels, and the follow-up newsletter that lands in their inbox. I need a graphic designer who can translate a high-end, luxury aesthetic into these three formats and keep the look seamless across every touchpoint. Scope of work • Design visually engaging travel-itinerary PDFs ready for print and digital distribution. • Create image assets that spotlight key moments of each trip for Instagram, Facebook, and our website banners. • Produce an email-newsletter template that matches the itinerary design so guests experience one consistent brand story. Style direction Think elegant typography, generous white space, and refined color palettes—nothing busy or overcrowded. Gold accents, muted neutrals, and full-bleed hero photogr

candidates.title: Modern Store Graphics Package
candidates.brief: I’m refreshing my online shop and need a cohesive set of graphics that instantly tells shoppers they’re in a sleek, modern space. The scope is focused on three key areas: • Store Banner – a hero image that sets the tone the moment visitors land in the store, sized for desktop and mobile. • Product Detail Page (PDP) assets – template-ready image frames, iconography, and typography treatments that make features, specs, and calls-to-action pop without clutter. • Store Decoration – background elements, section dividers, and accent graphics that tie everything together and keep the look consistent across every page. Expect clean lines, bold but balanced color, and plenty of negative space—nothing fussy or ornamental. Source files (PSD or AI) plus optimized JPG/PNG exports for immediate upload are required. Deliver layered files, clearly labeled, so my team can tweak text or swap images later.
